cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking how to save money when visiting Disney World according to the passage. First, I need to look at the options given: A, B, C, D. Let's check each one against the passage. The passage mentions that peak seasons are costly for accommodations. So option A says to go in peak seasons, which would actually cost more, not save money. So A is probably not correct. Option B says to stay outside the resort for a week or two. The passage states, "Staying outside the resort is a choice that may save you a large amount of money if you plan to stay for a week or two." That matches directly. So B is a correct point. Option C is buying food ahead of time in Disney World. Wait, the passage says "Another money saving tip is to buy food ahead of time. If you have most of your meals at your hotel or at cheaper restaurants, it will save you a lot of money. You can try not to buy food in and around Disney World." So buying food in advance, but not necessarily in Disney World. The option says "in Disney World," which contradicts the advice to avoid buying there. So C is incorrect. Option D is carrying enough food and drinks. The passage says, "Carrying food and beverages and enough cold water would help you reduce the cost, too." So D is correct as per the passage. So the question is asking which option is correct. The options are B and D.
